heading: General use of grants
label: 300ff–22 General use of grants

(1) to (4) as (2) to (5), respectively, substituted “therapeutics to treat HIV disease” for “treatments, that have been determined to prolong life or prevent serious deterioration of health,” in par. (5), and inserted after par. (5) “Services described in paragraph (1) shall be delivered through consortia designed as described in paragraph (2), where such consortia exist, unless the State demonstrates to the Secretary that delivery of such services would be more effective when other delivery mechanisms are used. children.text: core medical services described in subsection (b);
support services described in subsection (c); and
administrative expenses described in .section 300ff–28(b)(3) of this title

children.heading: Required funding for core medical services
children.label: b Required funding for core medical services

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: With respect to a grant under for a State for a grant year, the State shall, of the portion of the grant remaining after reserving amounts for purposes of subparagraphs (A) and (E)(ii)(I) of , use not less than 75 percent to provide core medical services that are needed in the State for individuals with HIV/AIDS who are identified and eligible under this subchapter (including services regarding the co-occurring conditions of the individuals).section 300ff–21 of this titlesection 300ff–28(b)(3) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: there are no waiting lists for AIDS Drug Assistance Program services under ; andsection 300ff–26 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: core medical services are available to all individuals with HIV/AIDS identified and eligible under this subchapter.

children.heading: Notification of waiver status
children.label: B Notification of waiver status
children.text: When informing a State that a grant under is being made to the State for a fiscal year, the Secretary shall inform the State whether a waiver under subparagraph (A) is in effect for the fiscal year.section 300ff–21 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Outpatient and ambulatory health services.

children.label: B
children.text: AIDS Drug Assistance Program treatments in accordance with .section 300ff–26 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: AIDS pharmaceutical assistance.

children.label: D
children.text: Oral health care.

children.label: E
children.text: Early intervention services described in subsection (d).

children.label: F
children.text: Health insurance premium and cost sharing assistance for low-income individuals in accordance with .section 300ff–25 of this title

children.label: G
children.text: Home health care.

children.label: H
children.text: Medical nutrition therapy.

children.label: I
children.text: Hospice services.

children.label: J
children.text: Home and community-based health services as defined under .section 300ff–24(c) of this title

children.label: K
children.text: Mental health services.

children.label: L
children.text: Substance abuse outpatient care.

children.label: M
children.text: Medical case management, including treatment adherence services.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “support services” means services, subject to the approval of the Secretary, that are needed for individuals with HIV/AIDS to achieve their medical outcomes (such as respite care for persons caring for individuals with HIV/AIDS, outreach services, medical transportation, linguistic services, and referrals for health care and support services).

children.heading: Definition of medical outcomes
children.label: 2 Definition of medical outcomes
children.text: In this subsection, the term “medical outcomes” means those outcomes affecting the HIV-related clinical status of an individual with HIV/AIDS.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “early intervention services” means HIV/AIDS early intervention services described in , with follow-up referral provided for the purpose of facilitating the access of individuals receiving the services to HIV-related health services. The entities through which such services may be provided under the grant include public health departments, emergency rooms, substance abuse and mental health treatment programs, detoxification centers, detention facilities, clinics regarding sexually transmitted diseases, homeless shelters, HIV/AIDS counseling and testing sites, health care points of entry specified by States, federally qualified health centers, and entities described in that constitute a point of access to services by maintaining referral relationships.section 300ff–51(e) of this titlesection 300ff–52(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Federal, State, or local funds are otherwise inadequate for the early intervention services the entity proposes to provide; and

children.label: B
children.text: the entity will expend funds pursuant to such subparagraph to supplement and not supplant other funds available to the entity for the provision of early intervention services for the fiscal year involved.

children.heading: Priority for women, infants, children, and youth
children.label: e Priority for women, infants, children, and youth

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For the purpose of providing health and support services to infants, children, youth, and women with HIV/AIDS, including treatment measures to prevent the perinatal transmission of HIV, a State shall for each of such populations in the eligible area use, from the grants made for the area under for a fiscal year, not less than the percentage constituted by the ratio of the population involved (infants, children, youth, or women in such area) with HIV/AIDS to the general population in such area of individuals with HIV/AIDS.section 300ff–11(a) of this title

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: 2 Waiver
children.text: With respect to the population involved, the Secretary may provide to a State a waiver of the requirement of paragraph (1) if such State demonstrates to the satisfaction of the Secretary that the population is receiving HIV-related health services through the State medicaid program under title XIX of the Social Security Act [ et seq.], the State children’s health insurance program under title XXI of such Act [ et seq.], or other Federal or State programs.42 U.S.C. 139642 U.S.C. 1397aa

children.heading: Construction
children.label: f Construction
children.text: A State may not use amounts received under a grant awarded under to purchase or improve land, or to purchase, construct, or permanently improve (other than minor remodeling) any building or other facility, or to make cash payments to intended recipients of services.section 300ff–21 of this title
